GIS in Airfield Maintenance

From Disconnected Databases to Federal Standards

←→
Scroll
Early 1990s
Airports Experiment with Spatial Databases

Airports use basic spatial databases for pavement records

Systems are proprietary and lack real spatial analysis

Pre-GIS Era
💾
1991
GIS Pavement System at JFK, LGA, EWR

Hanusel et al. prove GIS works for airfield pavement management

Sets the academic foundation for spatial maintenance

Seminal Research
1995
McNerney — 58% of Airports Plan GIS Adoption

McNerney proposes GIS for airport pavement and operations

58% of major airports plan to adopt GIS within three years

Seminal Research
2007 — 2009
FAA Launches Airports GIS Program

David Bennett leads development of federal GIS standards

GIS becomes a federal requirement for all certificated airports

Federal Standard
2013
Esri Releases ArcGIS Aviation Airports

Purpose-built GIS platform for airport operations launches

Enables pavement tracking, obstacle analysis, and asset management

Industry Milestone
November 2025
ArcGIS Aviation Airports v3.6

Enhanced tools for 3D surfaces and airfield marking management

GIS continues evolving as a live standard in airfield maintenance

Most Recent
